summary: Iteration 28 backend testing complete. 64/64 tests pass (33 regression from iteration_27 still green + 31 new tests covering BYOK credentials, deep-merge PATCH, async dispatch, /runs endpoint, route ordering, refactored auth routes, and engine v1.1.0). One real bug found and fixed during testing: motor AsyncIOMotorDatabase truth-testing crash inside lib/workflow_handlers.py::_load_byok was causing every BYOK action handler to crash with 'Database objects do not implement truth value testing' before it could check for a missing credential — fixed via `if db is None or not user_id`. All other refactors verified working: routes/auth.py extraction (login/me/register/forgot/reset all functional), routes/workflow_executor.py specific routes (/credentials, /jobs/{id}, /engine/status) correctly take precedence over /workflows/{id} catch-all (no longer swallowed as 'Workflow not found'), deep-merge PATCH preserves sibling headers, 50KB PATCH guard enforced, save validation rejects missing/whitespace studio_workflow_id with 400, action node dispatches to real Slack handler (returns error 'BYOK not configured' when no credential) but unknown services still skipped (v1 stub), async dispatch returns job_id immediately and asyncio.create_task background worker transitions queued→running→succeeded within ~1-2 seconds (well inside the 10s polling window), async dispatch enforces ownership on /jobs/{id} (404 for other user), template-execute now returns run_id and persists to db.workflow_runs with source='template', /workflows/{id}/runs lists owner-scoped runs (404 for non-owner), engine status reports version 1.1.0 with async_dispatch:true and byok_services array.
critical_code_review_comments: FIXED THIS ITERATION: lib/workflow_handlers.py::_load_byok used `if not db or not user_id` — motor's AsyncIOMotorDatabase raises NotImplementedError on bool() so the Slack/SendGrid/Gmail handlers would crash on the very first call. Replaced with `if db is None or not user_id`. Same pattern should be audited everywhere a motor db handle is used in a boolean context (rg 'if .*db' under /app/backend).

routes/workflow_executor.py is now 577 lines — still under the 700-line guideline but trending up. Consider extracting the async dispatch worker (_run_async_job + dispatch_workflow_async + get_job_status) into a dedicated lib/workflow_jobs.py before adding more job features (retries, cancellation, TTL cleanup).

Async dispatch uses in-process `asyncio.create_task` — acknowledged as v1 limitation in the docstring, but worth noting that a uvicorn worker reload or pod restart will drop queued/running jobs silently (status stays 'running' forever in db.workflow_jobs). Add a startup hook that marks stale running jobs as 'failed' with reason 'worker_restart' or a TTL field.

BYOK api_key is persisted in plaintext in db.byok_credentials. Acceptable for v1 BYOK but should be flagged in CHANGELOG and roadmap for encryption-at-rest (e.g., Fernet with a KMS-managed key) before any external customer enables this on real Slack/SendGrid/Gmail credentials.

POST /workflows/credentials request body is parsed manually from raw JSON — switching to a Pydantic model (BYOKCreate) would auto-validate types and produce structured 422s instead of ad-hoc 400 strings (which tests have to substring-match).

/workflows/{id}/runs returns whole node_results blob (including potentially large log strings) for each run with no pagination ceiling beyond limit=20. Add a 'fields' projection or a /runs/{run_id} detail endpoint and keep the list lean (id, success, duration_ms, created_at).

Auth routes/auth.py uses the lazy `_srv()` import pattern repeatedly per request — this works but is wasteful. A module-level import after server initializes (or stashing the resolved server module on the router state) would be cleaner.

success_rate.backend: 100% (64/64; 1 skipped on httpbin.org reachability — not a failure)

test_credentials: admin@nova.ai/admin123 (admin), freeuser@test.com/test123 (recruit, autouse fixture seeds to 100/100 for current period)
seed_data_creation: Per-test cleanup: TestBYOKCredentials autouse fixture wipes db.byok_credentials for both admin and freeuser before/after each test. All TEST_-prefixed user_workflows are deleted at end of each test. TestAuthRoutesExtracted::test_register_new deletes the synthetic user from db.users after assertion. db.workflow_runs and db.workflow_jobs accrue a small number of rows from synthetic executions — user-scoped, safe.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Two test files now cover the workflow executor: /app/backend/tests/test_workflow_routes.py (33 prior tests, classes TestEngineStatus → TestPatchNodeData) and /app/backend/tests/test_workflow_iter28.py (31 new tests, classes TestBYOKCredentials → TestRouteOrdering). When adding new endpoint coverage, prefer extending test_workflow_iter28.py or creating test_workflow_iter29.py rather than mutating the iteration_27 file. Conftest fixtures auto-login admin + freeuser and the autouse fixture seeds freeuser to 100/100. NOTE: I fixed one production bug this iteration (motor truth-testing in _load_byok) — main agent should verify they're happy with that 1-line change in /app/backend/lib/workflow_handlers.py line 188.
rca of the issue: BUG FOUND & FIXED: lib/workflow_handlers.py::_load_byok contained `if not db or not user_id: return None`. motor's AsyncIOMotorDatabase raises NotImplementedError when bool() is called on it (the well-known PyMongo/Motor caveat: 'Database objects do not implement truth value testing or bool(). Please compare with None instead.'). This caused every action node dispatched to slack/sendgrid/gmail to crash inside the handler before the BYOK lookup could even run, surfacing as 'Handler crash: Database objects do not implement truth value testing' in the execute response instead of the expected user-facing 'Slack BYOK not configured' error. Reproduction: POST /api/workflows/save with an action node `{type:'action', data:{service:'slack'}}` (and no BYOK credential), then POST /execute → node log shows handler-crash. Mitigation: changed to `if db is None or not user_id` (motor-safe). Verified by re-running TestActionNodeDispatch::test_action_slack_without_byok_returns_error — now passes with the correct 'Slack BYOK not configured' log message. Recommend grepping the rest of the codebase for similar `if not db` patterns.
